mechanism_narrative: Persistently low FEDFUNDS near zero compresses the equity risk premium by reducing the opportunity cost of holding stocks versus cash, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive during FEDFUNDS below 0.25% periods over any 2-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in PAYEMS signals weakening labor demand and falling aggregate income, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months where PAYEMS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising RSAFS and flat or falling INDPRO indicates demand is outstripping productive capacity, creating input cost pressures and margin compression that generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.98 as of October 2013 near capacity
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and INDPRO produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or returns are not predominantly negative when RSAFS rises and INDPRO falls.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL indicates low inflation, an accommodative FEDFUNDS level reduces real discount rates and supports valuation multiples, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During months with CPIAUCSL YoY change below 2.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO signal expanding industrial output and rising corporate sales, which predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not significantly positive over any 2-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: HOUST falling below 900 thousand units signals deteriorating construction demand and broader economic weakening, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months when HOUST is below 900 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in UNRATE and increase in RSAFS confirms a reinforcing cycle of job growth and consumer spending, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of falling UNRATE and rising RSAFS does not produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of coincident months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDPC1 growth below 2 percent signals insufficient aggregate demand relative to potential output, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of quarters when GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth is below 2.0%.
